top_signals.title: Oil Holds Decline as Traders Seek Clarity on Hormuz Reopening
top_signals.summary: Oil held its biggest drop in more than two weeks as traders, shippers and producers awaited details of a US-Iran deal that’s intended to pave the way for the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Netanyahu faces domestic backlash over US-Iran agreement
top_signals.summary: Prime minister launches a fierce defence of his wartime policies, insisting the strikes on Iran had been a success
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: An Oil Deal That Ended a War Nobody Won
top_signals.summary: What happened today is best described, despite much more dramatic categorizations coming out of Tehran and Washington, is a 60-day ceasefire extension, now reportedly signed by both parties, that reopens the Strait of Hormuz, while the original alleged reason for going to war, the nuclear weapons issue, stays open on the negotiating table. Both sides are buying time in order to get the oil flowing through Hormuz.  So, today’s deal is an oil deal, first and foremost. It’s not a victory for anyone. It’s just bringing things…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Average US petrol price falls below $4 on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: As cost at the pump declines to nearly two-month low, analysts warn risk of a surge ‘has not completely disappeared’
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
